edges.description: VCP dysfunction impairs maturation of ubiquitin-containing autophagosomes.
VCP-related multisystem disease includes abnormal bone remodeling.
Failed maturation leaves ubiquitin-positive cargo uncleared in autophagic vesicles.
Ubiquitin-positive vacuolar pathology in muscle produces the inclusion-body myopathy phenotype.
Ubiquitin/TDP-43 proteinopathy in brain contributes to frontotemporal degeneration.
ALS-like motor neuron disease is a multisystem proteinopathy manifestation downstream of VCP aggregate pathology.
Cardiac ubiquitin-positive inclusion pathology can manifest as heart failure in VCP disease.
Cardiac ubiquitin-positive inclusion pathology can manifest as cardiomyopathy in VCP disease.

nodes.description: VCP is required for autophagosome maturation under basal and proteasome stress conditions. Disease-associated VCP variants produce enlarged, ubiquitin-positive autophagic vesicles and patient-derived myoblast vacuoles, linking the upstream proteostasis defect to IBMPFD tissue pathology.

evidence.reference_title: VCP/p97 is essential for maturation of ubiquitin-containing autophagosomes and this function is impaired by mutations that cause IBMPFD.
evidence.snippet: Here we show that VCP is essential for autophagosome maturation.

evidence.reference_title: VCP/p97 is essential for maturation of ubiquitin-containing autophagosomes and this function is impaired by mutations that cause IBMPFD.
evidence.snippet: expression of disease-associated VCP mutants (R155H and A232E) also causes this autophagy defect.

evidence.reference_title: Inclusion body myopathy, Paget's disease of the bone and fronto-temporal dementia: a disorder of autophagy.
evidence.snippet: This is likely due to a defect in p97/VCP-mediated autophagosome maturation.

nodes.description: The bone branch of IBMPFD manifests as Paget disease, with abnormal rates of bone growth, osteolysis, elevated alkaline phosphatase, pain, enlargement, and fracture susceptibility.

evidence.reference_title: VCP disease associated with myopathy, Paget disease of bone and frontotemporal dementia: review of a unique disorder.
evidence.snippet: Paget disease of bone characterized by abnormal rates of bone growth that can result in bone pain, enlargement and fractures.

nodes.description: Skeletal muscle fibers develop a vacuolar inclusion-body pathology with rimmed vacuoles, elevated creatine kinase, myopathic EMG abnormalities, and progressive proximal and distal weakness.

evidence.reference_title: VCP/p97 is essential for maturation of ubiquitin-containing autophagosomes and this function is impaired by mutations that cause IBMPFD.
evidence.snippet: a defining feature of IBMPFD-related myopathy is the accumulation of large, ubiquitin-positive “rimmed vacuoles”

evidence.reference_title: VCP disease associated with myopathy, Paget disease of bone and frontotemporal dementia: review of a unique disorder.
evidence.snippet: Approximately 90% of the affected persons in the study have myopathy or muscle weakness particularly of the shoulder and hip girdles

nodes.description: No curative disease-modifying therapy is established for IBMPFD. Current care is supportive and multidisciplinary, with monitoring and symptomatic management of myopathy, respiratory compromise, bone disease, dementia, and cardiomyopathy as clinically indicated.

evidence.reference_title: Provisional practice recommendation for the management of myopathy in VCP-associated multisystem proteinopathy.
evidence.snippet: Standardized management of VCP myopathy will optimize patient care and help future research initiatives.

nodes.description: Impaired clearance of ubiquitinated autophagic cargo produces persistent ubiquitin-positive inclusions and vacuoles in affected muscle and brain, aligning the molecular defect with the hallmark proteinopathy.

evidence.reference_title: VCP/p97 is essential for maturation of ubiquitin-containing autophagosomes and this function is impaired by mutations that cause IBMPFD.
evidence.snippet: the autophagosomes that accumulate due to impaired VCP function contain ubiquitin-positive contents.

evidence.reference_title: VCP disease associated with myopathy, Paget disease of bone and frontotemporal dementia: review of a unique disorder.
evidence.snippet: Inclusions seen in the muscle, brain and heart in VCP disease contain ubiquitin, beta amyloid and TDP-43

evidence.snippet: VCP | valosin containing protein | hgnc:12666 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in

evidence.reference_title: VCP disease associated with myopathy, Paget disease of bone and frontotemporal dementia: review of a unique disorder.
evidence.snippet: Within 20 IBMPFD families whose data was analyzed for this study, ten missense mutations have been identified

nodes.description: Pathogenic VCP variants perturb the AAA+ ATPase functions that normally coordinate ubiquitin-dependent protein turnover and retrotranslocation of misfolded endoplasmic-reticulum proteins, creating the proximal proteostasis defect in classic IBMPFD.

evidence.reference_title: VCP disease associated with myopathy, Paget disease of bone and frontotemporal dementia: review of a unique disorder.
evidence.snippet: the VCP (p97 or CDC48) gene which plays a key role in the ubiquitin-proteasome dependent degradation of cytosolic proteins and in the retro translocation of misfolded proteins from the endoplasmic reticulum into the cytoplasm.
